name: payment-webhook-stripe-stale-sig
_note: Public route. A captured/stale Stripe-Signature (old t=) is rejected IDENTICALLY by both Node (stripe.webhooks.constructEvent, 300s tolerance) and Go (webhook.ConstructEvent, same 300s default tolerance) -> 400 {"error":"Invalid Stripe webhook signature","code":"invalid-input","request_id":"<uuid>"}, so parity holds WITHOUT a freshly-minted signature. request_id ignored (per-request uuid); error+code COMPARED. PREREQUISITES: 'stripe' in payment_methods_enabled AND the Stripe webhook signing secret SET in the shadow env (else both short-circuit to 201 {"success":false} — the unset-secret branch, also identical-on-both but a different assertion). To exercise the happy 201 path instead, regenerate the signature with a current t= (HMAC-SHA256 over <t>.<body> with the endpoint secret); captured webhooks always fail the tolerance window.
